message:
feat(chrome): improve tab bar rendering and expand E2E coverage

Tab bar improvements (crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/chrome.rs):
- Fix tab overflow: Calculate next tab width before rendering separator
- Prevent partial tab rendering by breaking loop if next tab won't fit
- Extract screen_registry into variable to avoid repeated calls
- Improve code readability with explicit width calculations

Session progress (SESSION_TODO.md):
- Mark Agent Mail tasks as attempted (blocked: MCP unavailable)
- Mark Shakespeare live search tasks as complete
- Update Code Explorer task progress
- Track bd-1e3w bead work completion status

New test coverage:
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/tests/screen_snapshots.rs: Add 67-line
  screen snapshot test suite for visual regression testing

E2E enhancements (scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh):
- Expand demo showcase E2E script with +189 lines of new test cases
- Add comprehensive screen coverage validation
- Improve test output formatting and reporting

Beads:
- .beads/issues.jsonl: Sync issue tracker with session progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

End-to-end validation of drag-and-drop functionality.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Mouse Drag (dnd_mouse_e2e.rs)

- Drag preview follows cursor

- Drop acceptance/rejection

- Navigate targets with arrows

### 3. Cross-Container (dnd_cross_e2e.rs)
- Drag between lists
- Type compatibility checking
- Drop position (before/after/inside)
- Drag into nested containers

### 4. Sortable Lists (dnd_sortable_e2e.rs)
- Reorder within list
- Visual placeholder
- Auto-scroll during drag
- Drag handle mode

### 5. Edge Cases (dnd_edge_e2e.rs)
- Drag cancel mid-flight
- Drop on disabled target
- Nested drop targets
- Long distance drag scroll

- INFO: Drag lifecycle (start, move, end, cancel)
- DEBUG: Hit testing calculations
- TRACE: Position updates per frame

 {"id":"bd-1eco","title":"Text Effects: Effect Sequencer / Timeline","description":"## Background
Complex animations require manual state management. A sequencer enables declarative animation timelines that automatically transition between states.

## Implementation

Add EffectSequence struct:
```rust
pub struct EffectSequence {
    steps: Vec<SequenceStep>,
    current_step: usize,
    step_progress: f64,
    loop_mode: LoopMode,
    global_easing: Easing,
    state: SequenceState,
}

pub struct SequenceStep {
    effect: TextEffect,
    duration_secs: f64,
    easing: Option<Easing>,  // Override global easing
}

pub enum LoopMode {
    Once,           // Play once, stop at end
    Loop,           // Restart from beginning
    PingPong,       // Reverse direction at ends
    LoopCount(u32), // Loop N times then stop
}

pub enum SequenceState {
    Playing,
    Paused,
    Completed,
}

/// Events emitted by sequence (instead of callbacks to avoid lifetime issues)
pub enum SequenceEvent {
    StepStarted { step_idx: usize },
    StepCompleted { step_idx: usize },
    SequenceCompleted,
    SequenceLooped { loop_count: u32 },
}
```

Builder API:
```rust
let seq = EffectSequence::builder()
    .step(TextEffect::FadeIn { progress: 0.0 }, 0.5)
    .step(TextEffect::Pulse { speed: 2.0, min_alpha: 0.5 }, 2.0)
    .step(TextEffect::FadeOut { progress: 0.0 }, 0.5)
    .loop_mode(LoopMode::Loop)
    .easing(Easing::EaseInOut)
    .build();
```

tick() returns Option<SequenceEvent> instead of using callbacks:
```rust
impl EffectSequence {
    pub fn tick(&mut self, delta_secs: f64) -> Option<SequenceEvent> { ... }
    pub fn current_effect(&self) -> &TextEffect { ... }
    pub fn progress(&self) -> f64 { ... }  // 0.0-1.0 overall
    pub fn is_complete(&self) -> bool { ... }
    pub fn pause(&mut self) { ... }
    pub fn resume(&mut self) { ... }
    pub fn reset(&mut self) { ... }
}
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_sequence_single_step: One step plays through
- test_sequence_multi_step: Steps transition at duration boundaries
- test_sequence_loop: Returns to step 0 after last step
- test_sequence_pingpong: Plays backward after forward
- test_sequence_loop_count: Stops after N iterations
- test_sequence_once_completes: SequenceCompleted event fires
- test_sequence_step_easing: Per-step easing overrides global
- test_sequence_event_step_started: Event fires at step boundary
- test_sequence_pause_resume: Progress freezes when paused
- test_sequence_reset: Returns to step 0, progress 0
- test_sequence_empty: Empty sequence is_complete immediately

## Integration
- StyledText::sequence(EffectSequence) applies current effect
- TransitionState can wrap a sequence for overlay animations

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Effect Sequencer / Timeline
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-1euu","title":"Widget Error Boundaries with Recovery","description":"# Widget Error Boundaries with Recovery

## Background & Motivation

Agent harness UIs often have complex widget trees that process unpredictable data (tool outputs, LLM responses, user inputs). A single widget bug should NOT crash the entire application.

**Why this matters:**
- Agent sessions can be long-running and expensive to restart
- Widget bugs may only manifest with specific data patterns
- Users need the UI to remain functional even if one component fails
- Developers need actionable diagnostics without terminal corruption

**Inspired by:** React Error Boundaries, Erlang supervision trees

## Technical Design

### Core Types

```rust
/// Captured error from a widget panic
pub struct CapturedError {
    /// Error message
    pub message: String,
    /// Widget that panicked
    pub widget_name: &'static str,
    /// Backtrace (if available)
    pub backtrace: Option<String>,
    /// Timestamp
    pub timestamp: Instant,
    /// Area the widget was rendering
    pub area: Rect,
}

/// Widget that catches panics from inner widget
pub struct ErrorBoundary<W: Widget> {
    inner: W,
    fallback: FallbackWidget,
    state: ErrorBoundaryState,
    config: ErrorBoundaryConfig,
}

enum ErrorBoundaryState {
    Healthy,
    Failed(CapturedError),
    Recovering { attempts: u32, last_error: CapturedError },
}

pub struct ErrorBoundaryConfig {
    /// Max recovery attempts before permanent fallback
    pub max_recovery_attempts: u32,
    /// Delay between recovery attempts
    pub recovery_delay: Duration,
    /// Whether to log errors
    pub log_errors: bool,
    /// Custom fallback widget factory
    pub fallback_factory: Option<Box<dyn Fn(&CapturedError) -> Box<dyn Widget>>>,
}
```

### Widget Implementation

```rust
impl<W: Widget> Widget for ErrorBoundary<W> {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        match &self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => {
                // Try to render inner widget
                let result = std::panic::catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(|| {
                    self.inner.render(area, buf)
                }));
                
                match result {
                    Ok(()) => {}
                    Err(panic) => {
                        let error = CapturedError::from_panic(panic, area);
                        self.handle_error(error, area, buf);
                    }
                }
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error) => {
                self.render_fallback(error, area, buf);
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { last_error, .. } => {
                // Show recovery indicator
                self.render_recovering(last_error, area, buf);
            }
        }
    }
}

impl<W: Widget> ErrorBoundary<W> {
    fn handle_error(&mut self, error: CapturedError, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        if self.config.log_errors {
            tracing::error!(
                widget = error.widget_name,
                message = %error.message,
                area = ?error.area,
                "Widget panicked"
            );
        }
        
        self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error.clone());
        self.render_fallback(&error, area, buf);
    }
    
    fn render_fallback(&self, error: &CapturedError, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        // Default fallback: red box with error indicator
        let block = Block::default()
            .borders(Borders::ALL)
            .border_style(Style::default().fg(Color::Red))
            .title("[Error]");
            
        let inner = block.inner(area);
        block.render(area, buf);
        
        // Show truncated error message
        let msg = format!("! {}", error.message.chars().take(inner.width as usize - 4).collect::<String>());
        buf.set_string(inner.x + 1, inner.y, &msg, Style::default().fg(Color::Red));
    }
}
```

### Recovery Mechanism

```rust
impl<W: Widget> ErrorBoundary<W> {
    /// Attempt to recover from error state
    pub fn try_recover(&mut self) -> bool {
        match &mut self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error) => {
                if self.config.max_recovery_attempts > 0 {
                    self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering {
                        attempts: 1,
                        last_error: error.clone(),
                    };
                    true
                } else {
                    false
                }
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { attempts, last_error } => {
                if *attempts < self.config.max_recovery_attempts {
                    *attempts += 1;
                    true
                } else {
                    // Give up, stay in failed state
                    self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(last_error.clone());
                    false
                }
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => true,
        }
    }
    
    /// Reset to healthy state (e.g., after model update)
    pub fn reset(&mut self) {
        self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy;
    }
    
    /// Get current error if any
    pub fn error(&self) -> Option<&CapturedError> {
        match &self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(e) => Some(e),
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { last_error, .. } => Some(last_error),
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => None,
        }
    }
}
```

### Composable Boundaries

```rust
// Wrap any widget in an error boundary
let safe_panel = ErrorBoundary::new(my_complex_widget)
    .with_max_recovery(3)
    .with_fallback(|err| Box::new(Text::raw(format!("Error: {}", err.message))));

// Nested boundaries for granular control
let app = ErrorBoundary::new(
    Column::new()
        .push(ErrorBoundary::new(header_widget))
        .push(ErrorBoundary::new(main_content))
        .push(ErrorBoundary::new(footer_widget))
);
```

### Integration with Model/Update Pattern

```rust
impl<M: Model, W: Widget> ErrorBoundary<W> {
    /// Clear error state when model changes significantly
    pub fn on_model_update(&mut self, model: &M) {
        if self.state.is_failed() && model.error_may_be_resolved() {
            self.try_recover();
        }
    }
}
```

## Test Plan

### Unit Tests
- [ ] ErrorBoundary catches panics without propagating
- [ ] CapturedError correctly extracts panic info
- [ ] Fallback widget renders in error state
- [ ] Recovery counter increments correctly
- [ ] Max recovery attempts respected
- [ ] reset() clears error state

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Nested ErrorBoundaries work correctly
- [ ] Error in one widget does not affect siblings
- [ ] Terminal state is not corrupted after panic

### Property Tests
- [ ] Random widget panics never crash the application
- [ ] Error state is always visually indicated
- [ ] Recovery attempts are bounded

### E2E Tests
```bash
# Test with widget that panics on specific input
./ftui-harness --test-mode error-boundary --panic-trigger "CRASH"
# Assert: UI shows error indicator
# Assert: other widgets still functional
# Assert: error is logged with context
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Panic capture (ERROR level)
tracing::error!(
    widget = widget_name,
    message = %panic_message,
    area = ?area,
    backtrace = ?backtrace,
    "Widget panicked, showing fallback"
);

// Recovery attempt (INFO level)
tracing::info!(
    widget = widget_name,
    attempt = attempt_number,
    max_attempts = max_attempts,
    "Attempting widget recovery"
);

// Recovery success (INFO level)
tracing::info!(
    widget = widget_name,
    "Widget recovered successfully"
);
```

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-2k018.3","title":"Implement table animation resolver (phase-driven styles)","description":"Purpose:
- Implement deterministic, phase-driven style transforms for table animation.

Implementation detail:
- Provide a resolver that lerps fg/bg colors based on a phase input (0..1).
- Use low-amplitude curves (breathing / pulse) to keep effects tasteful.
- Effects applied at row/column/section level only (avoid per-cell heavy ops).

Phase handling:
- Normalize phase via clamp/wrap semantics from spec (e.g., fract for >1).
- Apply per-effect phase_offset deterministically.

Suggested formula:
- breathing(t) = sin curve with asymmetry (quick inhale, slow exhale).
- alpha = min + (max-min) * breathing(t).
- style = lerp(base, highlight, alpha).

Performance:
- O(1) per row/column resolution; avoid allocations.
- No global time; caller passes phase (deterministic, testable).

Acceptance:
- Resolver produces stable output for identical phase inputs.
- Effect intensity is subtle by default (no visual noise).

 {"id":"bd-3bt4","title":"Create ErrorBoundary unit and integration tests","description":"# Create ErrorBoundary unit and integration tests

## Background
Part of Widget Error Boundaries (bd-1euu). Comprehensive tests ensure the error boundary works correctly in all scenarios.

## Unit Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    
    // Test widget that panics on demand
    struct PanicWidget {
        should_panic: bool,
    }
    
    impl Widget for PanicWidget {
        fn render(&self, _area: Rect, _buf: &mut Buffer, _budget: &RenderBudget) {
            if self.should_panic {
                panic\!("Intentional test panic");
            }
        }
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn healthy_widget_renders_normally() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: false };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        assert\!(\!boundary.is_failed());
        assert\!(boundary.error().is_none());
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn panic_is_caught() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        // Should NOT panic
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        assert\!(boundary.is_failed());
        assert\!(boundary.error().is_some());
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn fallback_renders_on_error() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 5));
        
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        // Check fallback rendered (red border)
        let content = buf.content_to_string();
        assert\!(content.contains("TestWidget") || content.contains("[\!]"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn recovery_works() {
        let mut widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        // First render: panic
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        assert\!(boundary.is_failed());
        
        // Fix the widget
        boundary.inner.should_panic = false;
        
        // Try recovery
        assert\!(boundary.try_recover());
        
        // Render again
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        // Should be healthy now
        assert\!(\!boundary.is_failed());
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn max_recovery_attempts_enforced() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget")
            .with_max_recovery(2);
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        // Initial failure
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        // Try recovery 3 times (should fail after 2)
        assert\!(boundary.try_recover()); // Attempt 1
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        assert\!(boundary.try_recover()); // Attempt 2
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        assert\!(\!boundary.try_recover()); // Should fail - max attempts reached
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn reset_clears_error() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        assert\!(boundary.is_failed());
        
        boundary.reset();

        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget")
            .with_fallback(|_err| Box::new(Text::raw("CUSTOM FALLBACK")));
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 5));
        
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        let content = buf.content_to_string();
        assert\!(content.contains("CUSTOM FALLBACK"));
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-3cc","title":"Implement Frame struct (Buffer + metadata)","description":"# Implement Frame struct (Buffer + metadata)

## Background
Plan Section 6.1 defines Frame as the container for a render pass: the Buffer grid plus metadata needed for presentation and interaction.

## Frame Purpose
Frame is the "render target" that view() methods write to. It bundles:
1. The cell grid (Buffer)
2. Optional hit testing grid (HitGrid)
3. Cursor metadata

## Structure

```rust
/// Frame = Buffer + metadata for a render pass
/// 
/// # Design Rationale
/// Frame is passed to Model::view() and contains everything needed to render.
/// The Buffer holds cells; metadata controls cursor and enables mouse hit testing.
/// 
/// Frame does NOT own pools (GraphemePool, LinkRegistry) - those are passed separately
/// or accessed via RenderContext to allow sharing across frames.
pub struct Frame {
    /// The cell grid for this render pass
    pub buffer: Buffer,
    
    /// Optional hit grid for mouse hit testing
    /// When Some, widgets can register clickable regions
    pub hit_grid: Option<HitGrid>,
    
    /// Cursor position (if app wants to show cursor)
    /// Coordinates are relative to buffer (0-indexed)
    pub cursor_position: Option<(u16, u16)>,
    
    /// Whether cursor should be visible
    pub cursor_visible: bool,
}
```

## Frame Lifecycle

```rust
impl Frame {
    /// Create a new frame with given dimensions
    pub fn new(width: u16, height: u16) -> Self {
        Self {
            buffer: Buffer::new(width, height),
            hit_grid: None,
            cursor_position: None,
            cursor_visible: true,
        }
    }
    
    /// Create frame with hit testing enabled
    pub fn with_hit_grid(width: u16, height: u16) -> Self {
        Self {
            buffer: Buffer::new(width, height),
            hit_grid: Some(HitGrid::new(width, height)),
            cursor_position: None,
            cursor_visible: true,
        }
    }
    
    /// Dimensions
    pub fn width(&self) -> u16 { self.buffer.width() }
    pub fn height(&self) -> u16 { self.buffer.height() }
    
    /// Clear frame for next render
    pub fn clear(&mut self) {
        self.buffer.clear();
        if let Some(ref mut grid) = self.hit_grid {
            grid.clear();
        }
    }
    
    /// Set cursor position (None to hide)
    pub fn set_cursor(&mut self, position: Option<(u16, u16)>) {
        self.cursor_position = position;
    }
    
    /// Show/hide cursor
    pub fn set_cursor_visible(&mut self, visible: bool) {
        self.cursor_visible = visible;
    }
}
```

## Integration Points

### With Model::view()
```rust
trait Model {
    fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame);
}

impl Model for MyApp {
    fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        // Draw widgets to frame.buffer
        // Register hit regions with frame.hit_grid
        // Set cursor position if needed
        self.status_bar.render(frame);
        frame.set_cursor(Some((self.cursor_x, self.cursor_y)));
    }
}
```

### With Presenter
```rust
// Runtime uses Frame for diff and presentation
let diff = BufferDiff::compute(&prev_buffer, &frame.buffer);
presenter.present(&frame.buffer, &diff, &pool, &links)?;

// Handle cursor separately
if frame.cursor_visible {
    if let Some((x, y)) = frame.cursor_position {
        presenter.position_cursor(x, y)?;
    }
    presenter.show_cursor()?;
} else {
    presenter.hide_cursor()?;
}
```

## HitGrid (Optional Feature)
Hit grid enables mouse interaction by mapping screen positions to widget IDs.

```rust
/// Hit testing grid for mouse interaction
pub struct HitGrid {
    width: u16,
    height: u16,
    cells: Vec<Option<HitId>>,
}

/// Identifier for a clickable region
#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct HitId(pub u32);

impl HitGrid {
    pub fn register(&mut self, rect: Rect, id: HitId) {
        for y in rect.y..(rect.y + rect.height) {
            for x in rect.x..(rect.x + rect.width) {
                if let Some(cell) = self.get_mut(x, y) {
                    *cell = Some(id);
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    pub fn hit_test(&self, x: u16, y: u16) -> Option<HitId> {
        self.get(x, y).and_then(|c| *c)
    }
}
```

```rust
        assert\!(frame.hit_grid.is_none());

        assert\!(\!frame.cursor_visible);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_hit_grid_registration() {
        let mut frame = Frame::with_hit_grid(80, 24);
        let hit_id = HitId(42);
        let rect = Rect::new(10, 5, 20, 3);
        
        if let Some(ref mut grid) = frame.hit_grid {
            grid.register(rect, hit_id);
            
            // Inside rect
            assert_eq\!(grid.hit_test(15, 6), Some(hit_id));
            
            // Outside rect
            assert_eq\!(grid.hit_test(5, 5), None);
        }
    }
}
```

The headless terminal must support these sequences used by FrankenTUI:

### CSI (Control Sequence Introducer)
- Cursor movement: CUP (H), CUU (A), CUD (B), CUF (C), CUB (D)
- Erase: ED (J), EL (K)
- Scroll: SU, SD
- SGR (Select Graphic Rendition): colors, bold, italic, underline
- Private modes: ?25h/l (cursor visibility), ?1049h/l (alt screen)

### SGR Attributes
- Reset: 0
- Bold: 1, Dim: 2, Italic: 3, Underline: 4
- Foreground: 30-37, 90-97, 38;5;N, 38;2;R;G;B
- Background: 40-47, 100-107, 48;5;N, 48;2;R;G;B

### OSC (Operating System Command)
- OSC 8 (hyperlinks): parse but don't render as link
- OSC 0 (window title): capture but don't use

### Unsupported (should not crash)
- OSC 52 (clipboard)
- DCS/APC sequences
- Unknown CSI sequences

```rust
impl HeadlessTerm {
    fn process_csi(&mut self, params: &[u16], intermediate: &[u8], final_byte: u8) {
        match final_byte {
            b'H' => self.cursor_position(params),
            b'J' => self.erase_display(params),
            b'K' => self.erase_line(params),
            b'm' => self.sgr(params),
            b'A'..=b'D' => self.cursor_move(final_byte, params),
            _ => {
                tracing::warn!(
                    final_byte = final_byte as char,
                    params = ?params,
                    "Unknown CSI sequence"
                );
            }
        }
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-3mo","title":"Implement markup parsing for styled text","description":"PHASE 4/EXTRAS - MARKUP PARSER (Feature-gated)

## Background
Markup parsing allows writing styled text with inline syntax like [bold]text[/bold] or Rich-style markup. Feature-gated as it's not needed by all apps.

## Technical Requirements

### Markup Syntax Options

#### Option A: BBCode-style
[bold]Bold text[/bold]
[italic]Italic[/italic]
[fg=red]Red text[/fg]
[bg=#00ff00]Green background[/bg]
[link=https://example.com]Click here[/link]

#### Option B: Rich-style (Python Rich compatible)
[bold]Bold[/bold]
[red]Red text[/red]
[on blue]Blue background[/on blue]
[link=url]Link text[/link]
[#ff0000]Hex color[/#ff0000]

### Parser Structure

pub struct MarkupParser {
    style_stack: Vec<Style>,
    current_style: Style,
}

impl MarkupParser {
    /// Parse markup string into styled Text
    pub fn parse(&mut self, input: &str) -> Result<Text, MarkupError> {
        let mut spans = Vec::new();
        let mut current_text = String::new();
        
        for token in self.tokenize(input)? {
            match token {
                Token::Text(s) => current_text.push_str(&s),
                Token::StyleStart(style) => {
                    if !current_text.is_empty() {
                        spans.push(Span::styled(
                            std::mem::take(&mut current_text),
                            self.current_style.clone(),
                        ));
                    }
                    self.style_stack.push(self.current_style.clone());
                    self.current_style = self.current_style.merge(style);
                }
                Token::StyleEnd => {
                    if !current_text.is_empty() {
                        spans.push(Span::styled(
                            std::mem::take(&mut current_text),
                            self.current_style.clone(),
                        ));
                    }
                    self.current_style = self.style_stack.pop().unwrap_or_default();
                }
            }
        }
        
        Ok(Text::from_spans(spans))
    }
}

### Error Handling
pub enum MarkupError {
    UnmatchedTag { tag: String, position: usize },
    InvalidColor { value: String },
    InvalidAttribute { name: String },
    NestedLinkNotAllowed,
}

### Extension: Emoji Shortcodes
Optional: :smile: -> 😀, :rocket: -> 🚀

pub fn expand_emoji(input: &str) -> Cow<str> {
    // Fast path: no colons
    if !input.contains(':') {
        return Cow::Borrowed(input);
    }
    // Replace :name: patterns with Unicode
}

### Integration
// Direct usage
let text = markup!("[bold]Hello[/bold] [red]world[/red]");

// Via Text
let text = Text::from_markup("[bold]Hello[/bold]");

// In widgets
Paragraph::new(markup!("[italic]Styled[/italic] text"))

 {"id":"bd-3o1l","title":"Text Effects: Mirror / Reflection","description":"## Background
Mirror/reflection creates a classic 'glossy' text effect with a vertically flipped copy below. Works especially well for titles using AsciiArtText, terminal-appropriate since it uses vertical space.

## Implementation

Add Reflection struct (separate from TextEffect for flexibility):
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Reflection {
    pub gap: u16,            // Rows between text and reflection (0-3 typical)
    pub opacity_start: f64,  // Top of reflection (0.3-0.5 typical)
    pub opacity_end: f64,    // Bottom of reflection (0.0 typical)
    pub height_ratio: f64,   // 0.0-1.0, how much of text height to reflect
    pub wave: f64,           // 0.0=perfect mirror, 1.0=wavy water effect
}

impl Default for Reflection {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            gap: 1,
            opacity_start: 0.4,
            opacity_end: 0.0,
            height_ratio: 0.5,  // Reflect bottom half of text
            wave: 0.0,
        }
    }
}
```

### Usage with StyledText:
```rust
// Only works with multi-line text (AsciiArtText or actual multi-line)
let art = AsciiArtText::new("TITLE", AsciiArtStyle::Block);
StyledText::from_ascii_art(art)
    .reflection(Reflection::default())
    .effect(TextEffect::HorizontalGradient { ... })
```

### Single-Line Behavior:
For single-line text, reflection is a no-op (nothing to flip vertically). Use AsciiArtText for reflections on titles.

### Rendering:
```rust
fn render_with_reflection(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame) {
    // 1. Render main text
    self.render_text(main_area, frame);
    
    // 2. Calculate reflection area
    let reflect_height = (self.text_height as f64 * self.reflection.height_ratio) as u16;
    let reflect_y = main_area.bottom() + self.reflection.gap;
    
    // 3. Render flipped text with gradient opacity
    for row in 0..reflect_height {
        let source_row = self.text_height - 1 - row;  // Flip vertically
        let opacity = lerp(self.reflection.opacity_start, 
                          self.reflection.opacity_end,
                          row as f64 / reflect_height as f64);
        
        let wave_offset = if self.reflection.wave > 0.0 {
            (row as f64 * 0.5 + self.time * 2.0).sin() * self.reflection.wave
        } else { 0.0 };
        
        self.render_row_with_opacity(source_row, reflect_y + row, opacity, wave_offset, frame);
    }
}
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_reflection_single_line_noop: No reflection for 1-row text
- test_reflection_multiline_flips: Rows rendered in reverse order
- test_reflection_gap_applied: Correct spacing between text and reflection
- test_reflection_opacity_gradient: Top brighter, bottom fainter
- test_reflection_height_ratio: Only reflects specified portion
- test_reflection_wave_offsets: Horizontal shimmer with wave > 0
- test_reflection_inherits_effects: Gradient text = gradient reflection

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot 5-row AsciiArt 'HELLO' with reflection
- Snapshot with wave=0.5 showing water ripple effect
- Snapshot gradient text with reflection (both should be gradient)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Mirror / Reflection
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
